SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.valve

Class ChokeCollapseResult

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.valve.ChokeCollapseResult
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ChokeCollapseResult extends Object implements Serializable
Result of a ChokeCollapseAnalyzer analysis.

Holds the diagnosed flow regime, collapse mode, and the numerical margins used to reach the verdict. The class is serialisable so it can be embedded in ProcessAutomation snapshots and equipment reports.

Version:
1.0
Author:
esol
See Also:

      public double getPressureRatio()
      Returns:
      actual pressure ratio p2/p1

    • setPressureRatio

      public void setPressureRatio(double r)
      Parameters:
      r - pressure ratio p2/p1

    • getCriticalPressureRatio

      public double getCriticalPressureRatio()
      Returns:
      critical pressure ratio r_c

    • setCriticalPressureRatio

      public void setCriticalPressureRatio(double rc)
      Parameters:
      rc - critical pressure ratio

    • getMarginToCollapse

      public double getMarginToCollapse()
      Returns:
      signed margin (r_c - r); positive means choked, negative means subcritical

    • setMarginToCollapse

      public void setMarginToCollapse(double m)
      Parameters:
      m - margin

    • getGamma

      public double getGamma()
      Returns:
      heat capacity ratio used

      public void setGamma(double g)
      Parameters:
      g - heat capacity ratio

      Parameters:

    • getMachNumber

      public double getMachNumber()
      Returns:
      throat Mach number estimate (1.0 at choke)

    • setMachNumber

      public void setMachNumber(double m)
      Parameters:
      m - Mach number

    • getCavitationIndex

      public double getCavitationIndex()
      Returns:
      cavitation index sigma = (p2 - pv) / (p1 - p2); NaN for gas service

    • setCavitationIndex

      public void setCavitationIndex(double s)
      Parameters:
      s - cavitation index
